# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Istio PeerAuthentication policy for our microservices namespace. Configure mTLS mode to allow both plaintext and encrypted traffic for backward compatibility. This is for our Kubernetes cluster during migration to service mesh.
# Category: istio_permissive_mtls

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: mtls-peerauthzpolicy
spec:
  selector:
    labels:
      app: myapp # your microservice label here, e.g., "myapp" or similar based on the application being deployed in Kubernetes cluster/context (e.g.: 'production', 'staging')    
---  
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1  // This should be v1alpha3 for ISTIO X, where as istio and mTLS are not supported by default in the same release version (e.g., alpha). So use this one or another depending upon your installation
kind: SecurityPolicy   
metadata:  
 name : my-migrationpolicy  //Give a meaningful name to policy for easy debugging later on - 'my_app' should be replaced with application context in production environment; e, g. "prod" OR similar based the deployment mode (e.,g.: staging) or use default value if not provided by Kubernetes/context
spec:  
 mtlsOptions:  //Specify MTLS options here for backward compatibility   
 - minVersion: V3_ALPN  # Support both plaintext and encrypted traffic    
 gateways: [ "*" ]      # specify gateway name as '*' to allow all by default. Replace this with your specific requirement in microservice or K8s namespace   // e, g.: myapp-gateway (your application Gateway label here)  if not provided it will use the context where command is run
